marketState: neutral-to-bearish, storage overhang offsetting a fading near-term heat pulse
summary: Natural gas is trading essentially at fair value ($2.867 vs $2.923 fair, FAIR status) with a fair-value curve that drifts up toward $3.017 by +21D, but that mild support is outweighed by a clearly bearish backdrop: storage sits +180 Bcf above the 5-year average after a larger-than-forecast +41 Bcf build (a bearish +22 Bcf surprise), and all six technical timeframes (5m-1w) show bearish trend with 50<200 EMA crosses. Seasonality is low-reliability and net soft, with price already running below its typical seasonal path (-8.9pp vs median, -15.8% YTD vs -6.9% median) and probHigher readings under 50% in 3 of 4 forward weeks (typical 4-week move -1.4%). Weather is the main near-term wildcard: 1-7D demand is running +19.5% above normal (rangePos 0.8, rising +15.9 in 24h), which should cushion the front month over the next week, but that heat pulse fades to near-normal by 8-16D (-0.6%, rangePos 0.51), leaving no durable medium-term tailwind. Managed money is net short (-60,295 contracts) but only at the 35th percentile of the 5-year range, so positioning is bearish-leaning but not crowded enough to argue for an imminent short-covering squeeze; futuresCurve data was unavailable so curve structure could not be checked.

longTerm.rationale: Longer-term drivers conflict: the fair-value curve rises from $2.92 today toward $3.02 by +21D (mildly constructive) and weather 8-16D is roughly neutral, but a 180 Bcf storage surplus with a bearish build surprise and low-reliability, slightly negative seasonality (typicalMove4w -1.4%, 3 of 4 probHigher windows below 50%) argue against chasing that drift. COT managed-money shorts (35th 5y percentile) aren't extreme enough to signal a squeeze either way.
longTerm.invalidation: A run of bullish storage surprises (draws vs forecast) or a break above the fair-value 'now' band's upper-middle (~$3.10) would flip this toward long; a confirmed weekly close below ~$2.65 with another bearish storage surprise would flip it toward short.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either a pullback toward $2.55-$2.65 (lower part of the fair-value band) for a value long, or a confirmed weekly close below $2.65 alongside a further bearish storage surprise for a trend short.

shortTerm.entry: $2.85 - $2.90 (current front / a bounce toward fair value $2.923)
shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; trim at TP1, trail remainder to TP2
shortTerm.rationale: All six technical timeframes (5m through 1w) show bearish trend with 50<200 EMA crosses, and this week's storage build beat forecast by +22 Bcf (bearish surprise) on top of a +180 Bcf surplus vs the 5-year average. Price sits right at fair value ($2.867 vs $2.923 fair, FAIR status), giving a short entry without chasing an already-extended move.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close back above $3.00 (above the fair-value 'now' spread and recent swing high), or the 1-7D heat/demand pulse (currently +19.5% vs 5y) extending and intensifying into next week, would invalidate the short.
